message:
fix(grpc): make HealthWatcher service-specific and suppress spurious version bumps

The HealthService previously bumped its global version counter on every
set_status/clear/clear_status call regardless of whether the status
actually changed.  This caused two problems:

1. Redundant wake-ups: setting a service to its current status still
   incremented the version, waking all watchers for a no-op change.

2. Cross-service false positives: HealthWatcher was version-based, not
   service-scoped—changing service "a" would cause the watcher for
   unrelated service "b" to report `changed() == true`.

Changes:
- set_status: compare insert return against current status; only bump
  version when the effective status actually transitions
- clear: skip version bump when statuses map is already empty
- clear_status: skip version bump when the service was not tracked
- Add watched_status() helper that resolves empty-string service names
  through the same path as check(), keeping watcher semantics consistent
  with the health check RPC contract
- HealthWatcher now tracks per-service status snapshot instead of global
  version, so changed() returns true only when the watched service's
  effective status differs from the last-observed value
- Fix test assertion: watcher_b.changed() is now correctly false when
  only service "a" was modified

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
